title: Vitest config references non-existent .env.test
description: setupFiles dotenv.config({ path: path.resolve(__dirname, '.env.test') }) — file does not exist (verified via `find` and `ls .env*`; only `.env`, `.env.example`, `.env.production.example` exist). dotenv silently no-ops on missing files so tests don't fail, but this masks the fact that test config silently relies on the dev `.env` (which contains production-shaped DATABASE_URL etc). A new contributor cannot reproduce tests in isolation without inheriting the dev DB. Also true for vitest.integration.config.ts.
suggested_fix: Create `.env.test` (gitignored) with a `DATABASE_URL` pointing to a disposable test DB (or remove the dotenv.config line entirely and use vitest `env:` block already set below). At minimum, replace the silent fallback with an explicit error or generate `.env.test` from `.env.example`.

title: MSW server installed globally with onUnhandledRequest: 'error' breaks all unit tests that fetch real URLs
description: `server.listen({ onUnhandledRequest: 'error' })` is registered for every test file in vitest.config.ts. But most API tests use `vi.mock('@/lib/prisma', ...)` so the SUT never hits the network — they pass. However, tests like `__tests__/hooks/use-queries.test.tsx` and `__tests__/components/error-handling.test.tsx` use `globalThis.fetch = vi.fn()` (line 22 of use-queries.test.tsx, line 128 of error-handling.test.tsx). When MSW is listening AND the test reassigns globalThis.fetch, MSW's request interceptor remains active for OTHER code paths, causing spurious failures or masking bugs. The `http.all('*', ...)` catch-all in handlers.ts combined with onUnhandledRequest:'error' means any fetch the SUT makes to an unmocked URL will throw — tests that try to test partial mocks by relying on fetch fallthrough will fail in confusing ways. Two configs with conflicting global-state assumptions (MSW vs explicit fetch mock) is a structural hazard.
suggested_fix: Either (a) move MSW setup into a separate setupFiles entry gated by an env var like `MSW=1`, or (b) drop `onUnhandledRequest: 'error'` and rely on the `http.all('*', ...)` catch-all to return 404. Pick one global interceptor strategy — not both. Document the chosen strategy in the test setup file header.

title: isValidCuid mock is wrong — used by mockDb POST handlers, breaks any test seeding cuid IDs that don't start with c-x
description: `isValidCuid` requires `/^[c-x]/` (no a/b/y/z). CUID2 standard IDs can start with any hex letter. Mock POST /api/work-packages only accepts IDs starting c-x even though real Prisma cuid() produces IDs like `cXXXXXXX` OR `clXXXXXXX` (Prisma's @default(cuid(2)) in Prisma 7 produces alphanumeric). This makes the MSW mock DB unusable for tests that want to POST a work-package then GET it back via the same handler — the mock POST will reject real cuid-format IDs. This is a brittleness bug (test mocks diverging from production schema).
suggested_fix: Replace the isValidCuid validator with a more permissive check (e.g. `typeof s === 'string' && s.length >= 20 && /^[a-z0-9]+$/.test(s)`) that matches both cuid v1 and v2. Add a comment noting this is a mock-only check, the real Zod schema lives elsewhere.

title: Tautological assertions in 'source-code verified' describe block — 4 tests pass with zero coverage
description: Lines 196–216 contain tests like `expect('text-red-500').toBeTruthy()`, `expect('text-red-600').toBeTruthy()`, `expect('h-48').toBeTruthy()`, `expect('overflow-auto').toBeTruthy()` etc. These are pure tautologies — they assert that a literal string is truthy, which is always true. They do NOT read the component source files they claim to verify. The comments claim 'Confirmed in WorkPackageTable.tsx' but no fs.readFileSync or import is performed. These 4+ tests provide ZERO coverage and inflate the test count by ~15 passing assertions.
suggested_fix: Replace the tautological tests with either (a) real source-file assertions: `expect(fs.readFileSync('components/work-packages/WorkPackageTable.tsx', 'utf8')).toContain('Failed to load work packages.')` or (b) delete the block entirely. Same for the 'Responsive adjustments' describe block at lines 220–247 which has the same pattern.

title: OAuth provider tests are tautologies — assert literal env-var name strings, not actual code/config
description: The entire 'OAuth provider environment variables' describe block (lines 80–114) tests that the string literal 'GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID' matches `/^GOOGLE_(CLIENT_ID|CLIENT_SECRET)$/` — a tautology. The 'OAuth account linking' block (lines 122–201) constructs plain JS objects in the test file and asserts on them — it never imports `lib/auth.ts`, never calls the real linking function, and never mocks anything. Lines 207–271 ('JWT callback OAuth provider tracking') also build a local MockToken and test inline if-statements, never touching the real `jwt` callback. This file's 272 lines provide essentially zero coverage of the OAuth code it claims to test.
suggested_fix: Either (a) delete the entire file and rely on integration tests, or (b) refactor to mock prisma/authOptions and import the real callbacks: `import { jwt, session, signIn } from '@/lib/auth'`. The current state is a 'coverage placebo' that will mask OAuth regressions.

title: Playwright retries=0 with no webServer config + workers=1 + fullyParallel=false
description: playwright.config.js runs E2E tests with `retries: 0` (no flake tolerance), `workers: 1` (no parallelism), and no `webServer` block (tests rely on human running `npm run dev` + `npm run dev:fe` first). There is exactly ONE Playwright spec (`frontend/test/e2e/phase0.spec.js`) covering one page-load smoke. The full login → create work-package → assign → complete user flow has zero E2E coverage. CI cannot run Playwright without a manual two-process setup script.
suggested_fix: Add a `webServer` array to playwright.config.js that boots both Next.js (`npm run dev`) and Vite (`npm run dev:fe`). Add a new spec `e2e/full-workflow.spec.js` covering login → create WP → assign → status change → comment. Allow `retries: 1` for headless CI stability.

title: work-packages-watch test only covers GET+POST; missing PUT/PATCH/DELETE/HEAD and 405 assertions
description: The watch route's `__tests__/api/work-packages-watch.unit.test.ts` only tests GET and POST methods. No 405 for PUT/PATCH/DELETE/HEAD. No boundary tests (very long subject strings, special chars in userId). No test for when `assigneeId === actorId` AND notification still fires due to assignee-change race. The announcement-route test (`announcements.unit.test.ts`) does cover 405s via withRoute HOF — this file does not.
suggested_fix: Add explicit `it('PUT /api/work-packages/[id]/watch returns 405', ...)` etc. Add boundary tests: empty `subject`, 10KB strings, unicode in assignee name. Test the 'watcher added but already watching' idempotent path.

title: Brittle mock implementation uses mock.calls.length to differentiate calls — coupling to invocation order
description: Lines 250–262: `mocks.workPackageRelationFindUnique.mockImplementation(({ where }) => { if (where?.id && mocks.workPackageRelationFindUnique.mock.calls.length <= 1) return ...; return ... })`. The helper's call is identified by 'mock.calls.length <= 1' — a global counter that resets between tests but couples this mock to the EXACT number of findUnique calls the handler will make. If the handler adds one more findUnique (e.g., a permission re-check), this mock silently falls through to the wrong branch. Brittle coupling to internal call ordering.
suggested_fix: Use `mockImplementationOnce` chained: `.mockImplementationOnce((args) => helperResponse).mockImplementationOnce((args) => detailResponse)` so each invocation is explicit. Same fix needed in `wiki.unit.test.ts` (which already uses mockResolvedValueOnce correctly) — apply consistently across all sprint B-N tests.

title: Brittle test for system-admin bypass asserts internal call-site details
description: Test `system admin bypasses both project and member lookups` (line 186–192) asserts `expect(mocks.projectFindUnique).not.toHaveBeenCalled()` and `expect(mocks.memberFindUnique).not.toHaveBeenCalled()`. These are white-box assertions about WHICH Prisma methods the helper invokes. If a future optimization pre-fetches project metadata for audit logging (a sensible change), this test fails even though behavior is correct. Brittle to internal refactors.
suggested_fix: Replace white-box call-count assertions with behavior-level: assert the response shape is identical to the happy-path-with-member case, plus assert that memberFindUnique is only called with admin=false (when isSystemAdmin=false). Move the call-count assertion to a separate `describe('internal: helper skips member lookup when isSystemAdmin', ...)` block so refactors are intentional.

title: No Phase 7 RBAC route tests for forum helpers (assertForumProjectMembership, assertThreadProjectMembership, assertPostProjectMembership)
description: The `__tests__/api/forums.unit.test.ts` file (603 lines) appears to be a Phase 4 baseline test (per AGENTS.md pre-existing baseline list). But the Phase 7 B-1 helpers `assertForumProjectMembership`, `assertThreadProjectMembership`, `assertPostProjectMembership` (referenced in AGENTS.md) have NO direct unit tests verifying 400/404/403/admin paths, and the forum route handlers have no withRoute-HOF integration tests like `wiki.unit.test.ts` has. This is a coverage gap on a critical Phase 7 deliverable.
suggested_fix: Create `__tests__/api/forums-rbac.unit.test.ts` (modeled on `wiki.unit.test.ts`) that exercises: (1) direct helper tests for assertForum/Thread/PostProjectMembership with the standard 400/404/403/happy/admin matrix, (2) 401/403/404/200 route integration tests via the withRoute HOF. Same for threads and posts subroutes.

title: Missing boundary tests: very long strings, empty agenda, invalid date range in meeting-create path
description: The meeting helper/handler tests cover 401/403/404/200 with happy-path bodies. They do NOT test: (1) empty title on POST (validation), (2) startTime > endTime (impossible meeting), (3) very long title (10KB) — would be a DoS vector, (4) agendaItemId of zero, (5) agendaDuration of negative number, (6) attendees array with 10000 entries, (7) attendees array with empty array. No boundary tests for any of these edge cases that real users / buggy clients will hit.
suggested_fix: Add a `describe('boundary cases', ...)` block with tests: empty title → 400; startTime > endTime → 400; title 100KB → 400 or truncation; 10000 attendees → 413 or graceful batching. Reference the Zod schema in `lib/api/withRoute.ts` to confirm expected error codes.

title: PUBLIC_GET_ROUTES allows /api/work-packages/[id] to be publicly readable — security regression
description: Line 48 of auth-guard.test.ts whitelists `'/api/work-packages/test123'` (i.e. `/api/work-packages/[id]`) as public-readable with comment 'Phase 0 design: WP detail page is public-readable (project visibility enforced elsewhere)'. This is a security hole — work-package detail often contains sensitive info (assignee, due dates, comments, attachments). Comment admits 'project visibility enforced elsewhere' but no test verifies that other-layer enforcement. Combined with the AGENTS.md note that the project is in 'Phase 7 RBAC Hardening' mode, this is a Phase 7 violation.
suggested_fix: Move `/api/work-packages/test123` OUT of PUBLIC_GET_ROUTES and assert it returns 401 like all other Phase 7-protected routes. If a separate public-by-design list is needed for a future 'shareable public WP' feature, gate it behind a separate config flag and explicit test suite.

title: Test depends on test execution order: mockResolvedValueOnce chain relies on call count 1 and 2
description: The happy-path 200 tests (lines 343–361 and 363–383) chain `mockResolvedValueOnce` calls assuming the helper calls `wikiPageFindUnique` FIRST and the handler calls it SECOND. If the handler is refactored to call `wikiPageFindUnique` before invoking the helper (a legitimate optimization), the chain breaks. Brittle coupling to call ordering within a single request lifecycle.
suggested_fix: Switch to a `mockImplementation` that branches on the `where` argument or the call argument shape (e.g., `select: { projectId: true }` vs full include), as `documents-meetings-search.unit.test.ts` does more cleanly. Or use `mockImplementationOnce` chained with comments naming the call site.

title: vitest.config.ts excludes auth-guard and routes-integration tests from `npm test`
description: The default `npm test` script excludes `__tests__/api/routes-integration.test.ts` and `__tests__/api/auth-guard.test.ts` — both are critical security regression guards. Developers running `npm test` will get green checkmarks WITHOUT exercising auth-guard coverage. The exclusion is documented as 'integration tests need real dev server' but a developer reading 'all tests pass' on a PR may not know to run `npm run test:integration` separately.
suggested_fix: Add a CI script that runs BOTH `npm test` AND `npm run test:integration` (and `npm run test:e2e`) in sequence. Or split `npm test` into `npm run test:unit` (only the default config) and `npm run test:all` which is the union. At minimum, add a banner warning when only one config is run.

title: mockDb initialized at module-load time and shared across all tests via globalThis singleton — state leak risk
description: `export const mockDb: MockDb = { ... }` at line 37 is a module-level singleton. `resetMockDb()` re-creates projects+workPackages but leaves statuses/types/priorities untouched (intentional? undocumented). The `afterEach(..., resetMockDb)` cleanup in setup.ts runs ONLY for tests using the default config (vitest.config.ts). Integration tests (vitest.integration.config.ts) also use src/test/setup.ts — so resetMockDb runs for them too. But if a future test imports handlers.ts WITHOUT going through setup.ts (e.g. by setting up MSW manually), it would share state with the rest of the test suite. The MSW handlers closure also captures mockDb by reference, so any reassignment to mockDb.projects in a test mutates shared state.
suggested_fix: Refactor mockDb to be created per-test-suite: expose a `createMockDb()` factory; let handlers.ts accept the db as a parameter; have each test file own its own instance. Or wrap the singleton in `vi.resetModules()` so each test file gets a fresh import.

title: No Phase 7 RBAC tests for documents/folders/helpers routes (assertDocumentProjectMembership, assertFolderProjectMembership) at the route level
description: `__tests__/api/documents.unit.test.ts` only tests the helpers directly — there are NO route integration tests (401/403/404/200) for `pages/api/documents`, `pages/api/documents/[id]`, `pages/api/documents/folders`, `pages/api/documents/folders/[id]`. Same pattern as the B-1 forum gap. The AGENTS.md says 'Route integration tests (401/403/404 via the actual handler) are shipped in the next commit (route migration)' in the documents test header comment — but no PR has landed these yet.
suggested_fix: Add route-level integration tests for documents and folders following the wiki.unit.test.ts pattern. Verify withRoute HOF is actually applied (i.e. the route file imports `withRoute`). Flag any route that still uses raw handler without withRoute as a separate finding.

title: 405 Method-Not-Allowed tests assume Allow header convention but never assert on res.headers
description: Lines 38–76 verify that POST/PATCH/DELETE on /api/statuses returns 405 but only check `res.status`. The handler sets `res.setHeader('Allow', ['GET'])` but no test asserts the Allow header is present. This is the standard HTTP contract for 405 — without asserting it, the test allows the handler to silently drop the header.
suggested_fix: Add `expect(res.headers.get('Allow')).toBe('GET')` (or equivalent for next-test-api-route-handler API) to each 405 test. Same for types and priorities.

title: 404 detection relies on Prisma error message string matching — brittle to ORM version upgrades
description: Lines 267–275 and 277–282 detect 'not found' by checking `mocks.announcementUpdate.mockRejectedValue(new Error('Record to update not found.'))`. This couples to Prisma's exact error message text. A Prisma 8 update could change this string and silently break the test (404 path goes untested). Brittle and not idiomatic.
suggested_fix: Have the route handler check `Prisma.PrismaClientKnownRequestError` with code 'P2025' (Prisma's official 'not found' code), and have the test mock the rejection with that typed error. Search the SUT source for the error-matching pattern and align tests to it.

title: No test for 'actor is system admin AND assignee changes mid-watch' — race condition untested
description: The watch handler has a notification-creation path that depends on whether the actor IS the assignee. No test covers: actor is admin, assignee is a different user, watcher count is 0 (so a notification IS expected to the assignee). Notification-create mock is verified to NOT have been called in some cases but the YES-case (admin adds watcher → assignee gets notification) has no test.
suggested_fix: Add `describe('notification fan-out', ...)` with: admin watches WP for user X → mocks.notificationCreate called once with userId=X; non-admin watches own WP → notification NOT created; non-admin watches other user's WP → notification created. Reference the SUT to find the exact branch logic.

title: routes-integration test depends on running dev server at TEST_API_URL=http://localhost:3001 — brittle and not in default npm test
description: `routes-integration.test.ts` uses `fetch(${BASE}/api/projects)` where `BASE = process.env.TEST_API_URL || 'http://localhost:3001'`. The integration config excludes this test from `npm test` (line 21 of vitest.config.ts: `'__tests__/api/routes-integration.test.ts'`). This means a new PR can pass `npm test` while this file has stale assertions or no longer matches the route signature. No CI check enforces freshness.
suggested_fix: Add a CI workflow step that spins up the dev server in a background process and runs `npm run test:integration`. Or convert these tests to direct unit tests using `next-test-api-route-handler` so they run in `npm test`.

title: Mock prisma factory defines inline data — diverges from real schema; no reset function for inter-test isolation
description: The mock factory inside `vi.mock('@/lib/prisma', () => {...})` defines mockAuthor/mockProject/mockFolder/mockDocument/mockMeeting/mockAttendee as inline consts. There is NO `beforeEach(vi.clearAllMocks())` declared at the top level of this file — only inside individual describe blocks if at all. If a test mutates a mock's resolved value and another test runs without resetting, results leak. Also, the inline data uses c(2), c(3), etc. that the helper uses; if Phase 7 RBAC helpers later require IDs matching specific patterns, these mocks break silently.
suggested_fix: Add a top-level `beforeEach(() => { vi.clearAllMocks(); /* re-seed defaults */ })`. Move the mock data into a `vi.hoisted` block (matching the pattern used in wiki/announcements/meetings tests) so the factory closure is robust to vi.mock hoisting.

title: auto-discovered routes test loop never asserts 401 for child routes like /api/projects/[id]/settings
description: The auto-discovery scans `pages/api/**/*.ts` and probes each route with synthetic IDs. But many routes are nested 3 levels deep (e.g. `/api/forums/[id]/threads/[threadId]/posts/[postId]`) — the substitution `[id] → test123` produces `/api/forums/test123/threads/test123/posts/test123`. If any of these nested routes exists but isn't in PUBLIC_ROUTES, it must return 401. The test passes today, which is correct, but there's no positive assertion that ALL nested routes are tested — just 'they returned 401'. A new route file added in a sibling directory tree might be missed by the discoverer if the recursion is broken.
suggested_fix: Add an assertion at the end of the loop: `expect(discoveredRoutes.length).toBeGreaterThan(140)` (current count of routes). And add a `describe('coverage: every route file is probed', () => { it('counts match pages/api file count', ... ) })` test.

title: ui-smoke flagged in baseline — not in scope but referenced
description: ui-smoke.test.tsx is in the AGENTS.md pre-existing baseline list (not to flag). But this file's pattern of large shallow rendering tests with mock-everything is replicated across `__tests__/components/work-packages-*.test.tsx`. These component tests mock react-query, UI primitives, AND next/router — meaning they test that the component compiles, not that it works.
suggested_fix: For each component test, add at least one test that exercises the real (non-mocked) data flow path: render with a populated MSW handler and assert user-visible behavior. Treat component tests as integration smoke tests, not render-count tests.

title: LDAP client test only covers creation, not bind/auth/search/error paths
description: 33-line file tests only `createLdapClient({...})` returns a defined object. No test for: bind failure, bind success, search with results, search with empty result, network timeout, malformed baseDn, invalid port. The full LDAP integration surface is untested at the unit level. Group-map and sync tests exist (per find_files output) but client.test.ts is a thin smoke that gives false confidence.
suggested_fix: Expand to cover: bind with wrong credentials → throws, search with results → returns parsed entries, search with no entries → returns empty array, ldapjs network error → propagates. Use `vi.mocked(ldapjs.createClient).mockReturnValueOnce(clientWithFailures)` to drive failure modes.

title: system-admin bypass tests don't assert that withRoute HOF's audit log fires
description: The 'system admin bypass' describe block (lines 431–445) verifies that admins get 200 but never asserts that withRoute's audit log callback is invoked. If a refactor breaks audit logging for admin paths, no test catches it. The same gap exists across all sprint B-N tests.
suggested_fix: Add a `vi.mock('@/lib/api/audit', ...)` mock (or whatever the audit module is named — search for it) and assert it's called with the expected action/user/resource for the admin path. Same for non-admin 403 paths (should NOT be audit-logged).

title: No Phase 7 RBAC tests for forums/threads/posts — entire Phase 7 B-1 deliverable may be untested
description: AGENTS.md states Phase 7 B-1 (forums) is the first sub-phase done. But searching `__tests__/api/forums.unit.test.ts` (603 lines, pre-existing baseline flagged) does NOT contain Phase 7 RBAC tests like `describe('assertForumProjectMembership helper', ...)`. The B-1 helpers `assertForumProjectMembership / assertThreadProjectMembership / assertPostProjectMembership` from `lib/auth/project.ts` may have ZERO direct unit tests, and the forum route handlers may have NO withRoute-HOF integration tests. This is the largest single coverage gap in the Phase 7 deliverable.
suggested_fix: Create `__tests__/api/forums-rbac.unit.test.ts` (mirror of wiki.unit.test.ts) covering B-1 helpers + forum/thread/post routes. This is the highest-priority gap to close.

title: Inconsistent cuid generation across test files — same c(N) helper redefined 8+ times
description: The `const c = (n: number) => 'c' + '0'.repeat(24) + n` helper is duplicated across `wiki.unit.test.ts`, `documents.unit.test.ts`, `meetings.unit.test.ts`, `relations.unit.test.ts`, `work-packages-watch.unit.test.ts`, `announcements.unit.test.ts`, `documents-meetings-search.unit.test.ts`, and others. Each file uses the SAME prefix, meaning `c(1)` produces `'c0000000000000000000000001'` in every file. If a shared mock invariant relies on the same `c(1)` resolving to the same project across files, it doesn't (each test file has its own vi.mock'd prisma). This is mostly harmless today but a maintainability footgun.
suggested_fix: Extract the cuid generator and common mock factories (projectFindUnique, memberFindUnique, etc.) into `__tests__/helpers/mockPrisma.ts` and import across all sprint B-N tests. Reduces ~200 lines of boilerplate per file.

title: Seed-lookup test uses testApiHandler which imports real pages/api/statuses → real Prisma client instantiated
description: `testApiHandler({ pagesHandler: statusesHandler, ... })` imports the REAL handler, which does `import { prisma } from '@/lib/prisma'`. Even with MSW active, the Prisma client is constructed (singleton), and at request time `prisma.status.findMany()` will try to hit the real DB. If no DB is available, the test fails with a connection error rather than the intended 200. Currently passes because a real Postgres is reachable at /var/run/postgresql, but this couples test runs to local dev infra.
suggested_fix: Either (a) move seed-lookup test to vitest.integration.config.ts and document the DB requirement, or (b) mock `@/lib/prisma` in this file too (using vi.hoisted like other tests) so it runs without infra.

title: beforeEach resets mockSession but doesn't reset vi.mock('@upstash/ratelimit') per-test
description: `beforeEach(() => { vi.clearAllMocks(); mockSession = { user: { id: c(3) } } })` clears mocks but the upstash Ratelimit mock's `mockImplementation` returns a fresh object each call (no shared state). This works because each request creates a new Ratelimit instance. But if a future test relies on Ratelimit state across calls (e.g. slidingWindow accumulator), the lack of reset will surface as flaky tests. Latent risk.
suggested_fix: Add `vi.resetAllMocks()` (heavier than clearAllMocks — resets implementation back to vi.fn() default) if cross-test state becomes an issue. Or document that `vi.clearAllMocks` is intentional.

title: Pre-existing baseline flagged but file still has uncovered RBAC negative cases (per task notes)
description: File is in the AGENTS.md 'do not flag' list (paired with stash p7-b3.4-ext). Not flagged as a new finding. Noting here for completeness — when the baseline is unblocked, this file should be reviewed for boundary tests around (projectId, roleId, action) tuples. Mentioned for follow-up, not flagged.
suggested_fix: When unblocking: add boundary tests for empty permissions array, role with no project, permission inheritance via role hierarchy, custom role overriding built-in. Coordinate with stash p7-b3.4-ext author.
